case.title: BENDIX AVIATION CORP. v. INDIANA DEPT. OF STATE REVENUE

opinion.h1: BENDIX AVIATION CORP. v. INDIANA DEPT. OF STATE REVENUE, 355 U.S. 607 (1958)
opinion.h4: BENDIX AVIATION CORP. v. INDIANA DEPARTMENT OF STATE REVENUE, INDIANA REVENUE BOARD, INDIANA GROSS INCOME TAX DIVISION. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIANA. No. 701. Decided March 3, 1958.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Nathan Levy, George N. Beamer, Richey W. Whitesell and James W. Oberfell for appellant.
Edwin K. Steers, Attorney General of Indiana, and Lloyd C. Hutchinson, Deputy Attorney General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
355 U.S. 607 CARLSON v. WASHINGTON. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF WASHINGTON. No. 286, Misc. Decided March 3, 1958.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
